raw_consumer_statement:
On or about XXXX XXXX, 2023 I answered an email from XXXX offering a special price on their product & I could cancel at any time. At that time it was specified that by signing up it was agreed that future shipments for {$40.00} would be sent unless canceled. 

The above statement is the problem. When I signed up, I took no notice of XXXX 's " Rules of Cancellation '', so on XX/XX/XXXX XXXX sent them an email thinking that this cancellation was sufficient. So when requested by Citi, I stated that I had no knowledge signing a subscription agreement, then later when I found that I did agree until cancelled, I reversed my previous statement. So Citi states in enclosed attachment : " due to conflicting statements ''. In stating that I did agree unless canceled I was merely correcting the statement in which I stated that I did not sign up for a subscription. Citi bases their decision on just this and not the overwhelming evidence which I sent to them. 

XXXX states that I emailed them stating that I could not locate my account requesting they cancel my subscription.This is a false statement. Attached is my email of XX/XX/XXXX telling me that they, XXXX, could not locate my subscription.So, instead of responding to the sender of this email, XXXX, they send their answer and all future emails to a very old email address never used but XXXX time several years ago that I never received. How can I cancel without their " rules ''. I tried to cancel by email every time that I refused a shipment from the XXXX which was XXXX times & received no response because it appears they were sending emails to " XXXX '' whoever that my be.
